Title: Offline action queue
Description: The moment the MV3 service worker terminates, every action sent to the controller is rejected with 'Extension not connected'. The controller should buffer actions per session id, return a QUEUED response with a position, and replay them in order when the extension reconnects. Add a max buffer size (e.g. 100 actions, 5 min TTL) with QUEUE_FULL rejection past the cap.

Title: MV3 service worker = single point of failure
Description: Chrome can terminate the background service worker any time. The 30s heartbeat means up to 30s of dropped actions per restart. Move the long-lived WebSocket into a chrome.offscreen document. The service worker creates the offscreen doc on first connect; the offscreen doc owns the socket and proxies action requests to the SW via chrome.runtime.sendMessage.

Title: Read-only operations attach the debugger
Description: inspect, screenshot, and evaluate all go through chrome.debugger, which shows the 'Chrome is being controlled' banner even when the AI is just reading the page. Add a readOnly: true flag on the action. When set, use chrome.tabs.captureVisibleTab and chrome.scripting.executeScript with world: 'MAIN' for evaluate. Fall back to CDP only for writes.

Title: No auth on the WebSocket
Description: Anyone with localhost access to port 9223 can drive the browser. The HTTP pollingSecret exists but the WS path doesn't enforce it. Require a bearer token on the first WS message (in addition to {role: ...}). Reject mismatched tokens with a 1008 close code. Add authToken to the popup config UI.

Title: No request idempotency keys
Description: Same action sent twice executes twice. An AI retrying after a network blip will re-click the Buy button. Add optional idempotencyKey on every action. The controller dedupes in-flight and recently-completed (last 5 min) requests with the same key. AI agents set idempotencyKey: hash(action + params + attempt).

Title: Add press_key action
Description: The test had to use evaluate to submit the YouTube form because there's no 'press Enter' action. Add POST /action {action:'press_key', params:{key:'Enter'}}. Backed by Input.dispatchKeyEvent via the already-attached debugger. Use the same charToKeyInfo table from the type action. Support: Enter, Tab, Escape, Arrows, F1-F12, Backspace, Delete.

Title: Shadow DOM landmark extraction
Description: The YouTube search input wasn't in the initial landmark list because it's inside YouTube's custom web component. Update extractLandmarks in content.js to walk element.shadowRoot recursively. Filter out closed shadow roots (can't introspect).

Title: Smart tab discovery / routing
Description: The AI has to guess which tab to drive. Add: find_tab {urlPattern?, titlePattern?, active?} returns the best match. click/type etc. accept tabHint: 'main' | 'last-active' | urlPattern instead of a hard tabId. Define 'main' as the tab whose window has the most non-chrome:// tabs.

Title: Server-push events from extension
Description: The extension can detect tab changes / navigations but can't tell clients. The controller already has broadcastToClients — wire it up to forward: tab_activated, tab_removed, navigation_committed (URL changed), download_started, crashed (tab crashed). Clients subscribe on the WS by sending {type:'subscribe', events:[...]}.

Title: Structured error taxonomy
Description: Errors are free-form strings. An AI agent can't reliably branch on them. Add an errorCode enum: NO_TAB, ELEMENT_NOT_FOUND, DEBUGGER_DENIED, PERMISSION_DENIED, TIMEOUT, INVALID_PARAMS, UNKNOWN_ACTION. Keep the human-readable error string alongside for logs.

Title: Action history + replay
Description: The popup shows the last result; the rest is gone. Persist last 200 actions to chrome.storage.local. Add GET /history on the controller. Add POST /action/replay/{id} to re-send a stored action. Useful for debugging and recovering from partial failure.

Title: Streaming endpoint for long flows
Description: For long-running flows ('wait for the user to log in'), there's no way to stream page events back. Add a server-sent-events endpoint at /stream that multiplexes every extension event. Clients open with EventSource('/stream').

Title: Schema validation in the controller
Description: The controller forwards anything; the extension validates. A typo'd action name wastes a round trip with a 502. Add a small JSON-schema validator (or hand-rolled check) on the controller. Reject with 400 {error: 'unknown action: clikc'} before forwarding.

Title: TypeScript everywhere
Description: Right now we rely on node --check + careful coding. A build step with tsc (or tsx for no-build) would catch the insp.result.url vs insp.url class of bug at compile time. Start with the controller-server, then background.js, then content.js.

Title: Real regression test suite
Description: test-minimax-agent.js is a one-shot demo. A real suite (Vitest) covering: coordinate-scaling math (all 4 modes, DPR edge cases), protocol round-trip, per-action execution path, error taxonomy codes — would catch breakage on refactor.

Title: Metrics endpoint
Description: No counter for actions-per-second, error rates, p99 latencies. The log is rich but not queryable. Add a tiny /metrics endpoint in Prometheus text format: agent_actions_total{action='click',result='ok'} 42 etc.

Title: Popup UI polish
Description: Functional, but a real product needs a sidebar layout, history view, per-action latency display, connection-uptime counter.

Title: Auto-reload file filter
Description: I excluded .swp, ~, etc. but real projects have *.lock, .turbo/, dist/, coverage/, .git/, node_modules/. Make the ignore list configurable with a default that reads .gitignore.

Title: WebSocket backpressure
Description: A client that fires 1000 actions/sec will pile them up. Per-client in-flight cap (default 16) with QUEUE_FULL rejection. Sends a {type:'backpressure'} event when the cap is hit.

Title: Screenshot format consistency
Description: Page.captureScreenshot returns PNG only. chrome.tabs.captureVisibleTab supports PNG and JPEG. The 47 KB JPEG screenshots are ~3x smaller than the equivalent PNG. Add format: 'jpeg' | 'png' | 'webp' (with quality for jpeg/webp) to the screenshot action.
